cases.title: Client calls a service RPC and receives a successful typed response
cases.description: A client participant calls a service-owned RPC through generated surfaces and receives the typed successful response.

given: a live Trellis runtime
the rpc fixture contracts are approved
when: the client calls Entity.Get through the generated RPC surface
then: the service receives the typed input
the client receives { id, found: true }

cases.title: Service RPC handler receives authenticated caller context
cases.description: The service RPC handler observes non-empty caller, session, request, and trace metadata from the live request context.

given: a live Trellis runtime
the rpc fixture contracts are approved
when: the client calls Entity.Get through the generated RPC surface
then: the service handler observes non-empty caller metadata
the service handler observes non-empty session metadata
the service handler observes non-empty request metadata
the service handler observes non-empty trace metadata

cases.title: Client receives a declared service RPC error through the result surface
cases.description: The client calls an RPC whose handler returns a declared error, and the client result surface preserves the declared error code and data.

given: a live Trellis runtime
the rpc fixture contracts include a declared RPC error
when: the client calls an RPC whose handler returns the declared error
then: the client result surface preserves the declared error code
the client result surface preserves the declared error data

cases.title: RPC call is denied for a client without call authority
cases.description: A client lacking rpc.call authority for Entity.Get receives a denied result when attempting to call the RPC.

given: a live Trellis runtime
the client lacks rpc.call authority for Entity.Get
when: the client calls Entity.Get through the generated RPC surface
then: the generated RPC call is denied

cases.title: Publisher sends an event and subscriber receives the exact payload
cases.description: An authorized publisher sends a contract event and an authorized subscriber receives the exact payload with context id and time.

given: a subscriber is connected and listening for Entity.Changed events
when: the publisher sends an Entity.Changed event
then: the subscriber receives the exact payload
the received event contains context with id and time

cases.title: Event publish is denied for a subscribe-only client
cases.description: A client with subscribe-only authority attempts to publish a contract event and receives a public denied failure.

given: a client is connected with subscribe-only authority
when: the client attempts to publish an Entity.Changed event
then: the publish call is denied with a public denied failure

cases.title: Event subscribe does not deliver to a client without subscribe authority
cases.description: A client lacking subscribe authority attempts to listen for events and does not receive events published by an authorized client.

given: a client is connected without subscribe authority
when: the client attempts to listen for events
an authorized client publishes an event
then: the unauthorized listener does not receive the event

cases.title: Client starts an operation through public operation APIs
cases.description: An authorized client starts Entity.Process and receives an operation reference with typed input received by the service handler.

given: a service is connected and handles Entity.Process operation
when: the client starts an Entity.Process operation
then: the client receives an operation reference and id
the service handler receives typed input

cases.title: Client watches operation progress events
cases.description: The client watch stream observes at least one typed progress event with expected payload during operation execution.

given: a service is connected and handles Entity.Process operation
when: the client starts Entity.Process and watches the operation
then: the watch stream observes at least one typed progress event

cases.title: Client waits for operation completion and receives typed output
cases.description: The client wait helper observes terminal completed state with typed operation output.

given: a service is connected and handles Entity.Process operation
when: the client starts Entity.Process and waits for completion
then: the client observes terminal completed state
the client receives typed operation output

cases.title: Operation start is denied for a client without call authority
cases.description: A client lacking operation call authority attempts to start Entity.Process and receives a public denied failure.

given: a client is connected without operation call authority
when: the client attempts to start Entity.Process
then: the operation start is denied with a public denied failure

cases.title: Client receives the first feed frame after subscribing
cases.description: An authorized client subscribes to Entity.Live and receives the first typed frame for the requested topic.

given: a service is connected that handles Entity.Live feed subscriptions
when: the client subscribes to Entity.Live
then: the client receives the first typed frame

cases.title: Client receives feed frames in order
cases.description: The service emits two frames and the client observes sequence 1 followed by sequence 2 without reordering.

given: a service is connected that handles Entity.Live feed subscriptions
when: the service emits two feed frames
then: the client observes sequence 1
the client observes sequence 2 in order

cases.title: Aborting a feed subscription stops the stream without leaking
cases.description: The client aborts a feed subscription and the stream terminates cleanly without leaking a pending task.

given: a client has an active feed subscription
when: the client aborts the subscription
then: the stream terminates without leaking

cases.title: Feed subscribe is denied for a client without authority
cases.description: A client lacking feed subscribe authority attempts to subscribe and receives a public denied failure.

given: a client is connected without feed subscribe authority
when: the client attempts to subscribe to Entity.Live
then: the subscribe call is denied with a public denied failure

cases.title: Reading an empty value store returns found false
cases.description: Reading from a fresh value store that has no entry returns { found: false }.

given: a client is connected with a value store
when: the client reads a value that does not exist
then: the client receives { found: false }

cases.title: Value store create, read, and delete cycle
cases.description: Creating with expectedRevision null applies, read returns the value, revision delete succeeds, and final read returns found false.

given: a client is connected with a value store
when: the client creates an entry with expectedRevision null
the client reads the entry
the client deletes using the returned revision
the client reads the entry again
then: the create applies with a revision
the read returns the stored value
the delete returns deleted true
the final read returns found false

cases.title: Value store rejects stale revision writes and deletes
cases.description: Writing or deleting with a stale revision returns a public conflict or not-applied shape.

given: a client is connected with a value store
an entry exists with a known revision
when: the client writes with a stale revision
the client deletes with a stale revision
then: the write returns a conflict or not-applied shape
the delete returns a conflict or not-applied shape

cases.title: Map store prefix put, get, list, and delete cycle
cases.description: Using a prefixed map handle, writes apply, get returns the value, bounded list includes the key, revision delete succeeds, and final get is missing.

given: a client is connected with a map store
when: the client writes a key-value pair to a prefixed map
the client gets the written key
the client lists entries with a limit
the client deletes the entry with a revision
the client gets the key again
then: the write applies with a revision
the get returns the stored value
the list includes the written key
the delete succeeds
the final get returns missing

cases.title: Map store list respects the requested limit
cases.description: Putting more entries than the requested limit returns no more than limit entries with stable keys.

given: a client is connected with a map store
when: the client puts more entries than the requested list limit
the client lists entries with the limit
then: the list returns no more than the requested limit entries
the returned keys are stable

cases.title: Client uploads a file through the transfer operation
cases.description: The client sends bytes through the generated transfer operation and the service observes completed upload with size, key, and contentType.

given: a service is connected that handles file uploads
when: the client sends bytes through the transfer operation
then: the service observes completed upload size
the service observes completed upload key
the service observes completed upload contentType
the terminal output matches the upload

cases.title: Client downloads a file through a receive grant
cases.description: The service creates a receive grant for the caller session and the client downloads bytes through the public transfer helper with matching payload.

given: a service is connected that handles file downloads
a file is available for download via a receive grant
when: the service creates a receive grant for the client session
the client downloads bytes through the transfer helper
then: the downloaded payload matches the expected content

cases.title: Download grant is bound to the requesting session
cases.description: A second client attempting to use another client's receive grant receives a public denied or invalid grant failure.

given: a receive grant exists for the first client session
when: the second client attempts to use the first client's receive grant
then: the second client receives a public denied or invalid grant failure

cases.title: Service receives required resource bindings
cases.description: The connected service has required KV and store handles materialized through public service handles.

given: a service is connected with required KV and store bindings declared
when: the service connects to Trellis
then: the required KV handle is materialized
the required store handle is materialized

cases.title: Service receives optional resource bindings
cases.description: The connected service has optional KV and store handles present when declared and provisioned.

given: a service is connected with optional KV and store bindings declared
when: the service connects to Trellis
then: the optional KV handle is present
the optional store handle is present

cases.title: Service-bound store supports create, read, list, and delete
cases.description: The service uses a bound store during a client RPC and create, wait, read, status, list, and delete all work.

given: a service is connected with a store binding
a client can trigger an RPC on the service
when: the client calls an RPC that triggers store operations on the service
then: the service store create works
the service store wait works
the service store read works
the service store status works
the service store list works
the service store delete works

cases.title: Service-bound KV supports create, put, get, and delete
cases.description: The service uses a bound KV store during a client RPC and create, put, get, and revision delete all work.

given: a service is connected with a KV binding
a client can trigger an RPC on the service
when: the client calls an RPC that triggers KV operations on the service
then: the service KV create works
the service KV put works
the service KV get works
the service KV revision delete works

cases.title: Service-bound KV rejects stale revision operations
cases.description: The service attempts KV update or delete with a stale revision and the public handle surface fails.

given: a service is connected with a KV binding
a key exists with a known revision
when: the service attempts to update with a stale revision
the service attempts to delete with a stale revision
then: the stale update fails through the public handle surface
the stale delete fails through the public handle surface

cases.title: Service creates a local job in response to a client RPC
cases.description: The client RPC reaches the service, the service creates a service-local job, and the response includes a non-empty job id.

given: a service is connected that handles jobs
when: the client calls an RPC that triggers a local job
then: the response includes a non-empty job id

cases.title: Job progress and log entries are published
cases.description: The job handler publishes progress and log entries which are observable through the public job observation surface.

given: a job handler that publishes progress and log entries
when: the job executes
then: progress entries are observable through the public surface
log entries are observable through the public surface

cases.title: Job wait returns terminal state with typed result
cases.description: Waiting for a completed job returns terminal completed state with typed result.

given: a service is connected and handles jobs
when: a job completes
the client waits for the job
then: the wait returns completed terminal state
the wait returns a typed result

cases.title: Job context propagates request id and trace id
cases.description: The job response contains a non-empty request id and a 32-character trace id propagated from the client-visible workflow.

given: a service is connected that handles jobs
when: a client-visible workflow completes
then: the response contains a non-empty request id
the response contains a 32-character trace id

cases.title: Client subscribes to service health heartbeats
cases.description: An authorized observer subscribes to Health.Heartbeat and receives a heartbeat for the service.

given: a service is connected that emits health heartbeats
when: the observer subscribes to Health.Heartbeat
then: the observer receives a heartbeat for the service

cases.title: Heartbeat includes service identity metadata
cases.description: The heartbeat includes status, service kind, name, contract id, digest, runtime, version, instance id, startedAt, and publish interval.

given: a service is connected that emits health heartbeats
when: the observer receives a heartbeat
then: the heartbeat includes status
the heartbeat includes service kind
the heartbeat includes service name
the heartbeat includes contract id
the heartbeat includes digest
the heartbeat includes runtime
the heartbeat includes version
the heartbeat includes instance id
the heartbeat includes startedAt
the heartbeat includes publish interval

cases.title: Heartbeat includes built-in and custom health checks
cases.description: The heartbeat includes the built-in nats ok check and a custom fixture ok check with summary and info.

given: a service is connected that emits health heartbeats with custom checks
when: the observer receives a heartbeat
then: the heartbeat includes a built-in nats ok check
the heartbeat includes a custom fixture ok check with summary and info

cases.title: Heartbeat event context is populated
cases.description: The heartbeat event context has subject, mode, id, and time populated, with subject events.v1.Health.Heartbeat.

given: a service is connected that emits health heartbeats
when: the observer receives a heartbeat
then: the event context subject is populated
the event context mode is populated
the event context id is populated
the event context time is populated
the event context subject is events.v1.Health.Heartbeat

cases.title: Pre-approved service contract connects without a pending authority plan
cases.description: A service contract approved before startup connects, handles an RPC, and leaves no pending authority plans.

given: a strict deployment has accepted the base service contract
when: the service connects and the client calls Plan.Ping
then: the RPC succeeds
no pending authority plan remains for the deployment

cases.title: Presented additive contract creates a pending update and blocks connect
cases.description: A service presents a same-lineage additive contract at connect time; Trellis records a pending update plan and the service remains blocked.

given: a strict deployment has accepted the base contract
a service instance presents an additive same-contract digest
when: the service attempts to connect
then: the connect remains pending
a pending update plan exists for the additive digest
the added RPC is not mounted

cases.title: Accepted additive update unblocks service connect
cases.description: A pending additive update is accepted, materialized, and the waiting service connects and serves the added RPC.

given: a pending additive update plan exists
when: the update plan is accepted and materialized
then: the plan is listed as accepted
the service connect resolves
the client can call Plan.AddedPing

cases.title: Rejected additive update remains blocked and preserves old authority
cases.description: Rejecting a pending additive update keeps the new service blocked while the old approved contract path remains usable.

given: a pending additive update plan exists
when: the update plan is rejected
then: the plan is listed as rejected with the supplied reason
the additive service remains blocked
the base contract RPC still works

cases.title: Accepted incompatible migration enables same-contract replacement
cases.description: An incompatible same-contract replacement creates a migration plan; explicit acceptance allows the replacement service to connect and handle RPC.

given: a strict deployment has accepted and used the base digest
a replacement service presents an incompatible same-contract digest
when: the migration plan is accepted with acknowledgement
then: the replacement service connects
the replacement RPC succeeds

cases.title: Rejected incompatible migration keeps old contract usable
cases.description: Rejecting an incompatible same-contract migration keeps the replacement blocked and preserves the old approved runtime path.

given: a strict deployment has accepted and used the base digest
a replacement service presents an incompatible same-contract digest
when: the migration plan is rejected
then: the replacement service remains blocked
the old base RPC still succeeds

cases.title: Strict deployment allows compatible metadata replacement without manual approval
cases.description: A metadata-only same-contract replacement connects in strict mode without manual plan acceptance and leaves no pending plan.

given: a strict deployment has accepted and used the base digest
when: a metadata-only replacement service connects
then: the replacement connects without manual acceptance
the RPC succeeds
no pending replacement plan remains

cases.title: Mutable-dev auto-accepts incompatible same-contract migration
cases.description: A mutable-dev deployment records and auto-accepts an incompatible same-contract migration, allowing the replacement service to connect.

given: a mutable-dev deployment has accepted and used the base digest
when: a replacement service presents an incompatible same-contract digest
then: an accepted migration plan is listed
the replacement service connects
the replacement RPC succeeds

cases.title: Mutable-dev does not ignore an explicitly rejected update
cases.description: An additive update in mutable-dev can be rejected and remains blocked, proving auto-accept is limited to same-contract migrations.

given: a mutable-dev deployment has accepted the base contract
an additive update plan is pending
when: the update plan is rejected
then: the plan remains rejected
the additive service remains blocked

cases.title: Accepted resource migration binds the changed resource definition
cases.description: A changed required KV resource definition creates a migration plan; after acceptance, the replacement service receives a usable bound KV handle.

given: a strict deployment has accepted and connected the base resource contract
a replacement service presents a changed required KV definition
when: the migration plan is accepted and materialized
then: the replacement service connects with a KV binding
the client RPC proves the bound resource handle works

cases.title: Service startup blocks before deployment authority approval
cases.description: A service instance key is provisioned before contract approval, and the service connect remains pending.

given: a service instance key is provisioned before contract approval
when: the service attempts to connect
then: the service connect remains pending

cases.title: Service startup completes after deployment authority approval
cases.description: After the admin approves and reconciles, the pending service connect resolves successfully.

given: a service connect is pending
when: the admin approves the service contract
the admin reconciles deployment authority
then: the pending service connect resolves

cases.title: Approved service handles a client RPC
cases.description: An approved service registers an RPC handler and an authorized client receives a typed response.

given: a service is connected and approved
the service registers an RPC handler
when: the client calls the service RPC
then: the client receives a typed response

cases.title: App client connect triggers auth-required callback before approval
cases.description: TrellisClient.connect invoked before approval triggers an auth-required callback with a login URL, session key mode, and session key.

given: the app client contract requires identity grant approval
when: the client attempts to connect before approval
then: the client receives an auth-required callback
the callback includes a login URL
the callback includes a session key mode
the callback includes a session key

cases.title: Approved app client connects successfully
cases.description: After admin-assisted approval completes, the client connects with an open connection.

given: the app client contract requires identity grant approval
when: the admin completes the approval flow
the client connects
then: the client connects with an open connection

cases.title: Approved app client calls a service RPC
cases.description: An approved app client calls a generated service RPC and receives a typed response.

given: a service is connected and handles an RPC
the client has an approved identity grant
when: the client calls the generated service RPC
then: the client receives a typed response

cases.title: Admin provisions a known device identity
cases.description: The admin creates a device deployment, approves the device contract, provisions the device public identity and activation key, and receives expected instance metadata.

given: a deployment authority is configured
when: the admin creates a device deployment
the admin approves the device contract
the admin provisions the device with public identity and activation key
then: the admin receives expected instance metadata
the admin receives a deployment id
the admin receives a public identity key

cases.title: Device starts an activation request
cases.description: The device builds a signed activation payload, starts the activation request through the public HTTP helper, and receives an activation URL with flow id.

given: a device has an activation key and public identity key
when: the device builds a signed activation payload
the device starts the activation request
then: the device receives an activation URL
the activation URL includes a flow id

cases.title: Admin resolves the device activation operation
cases.description: The admin starts Auth.DeviceUserAuthorities.Resolve operation and it completes with activated status, deployment id, and instance id.

given: a device activation request is pending
when: the admin starts the resolve operation
then: the operation completes with activated status
the operation completes with a deployment id
the operation completes with an instance id

cases.title: Device receives connection information after activation
cases.description: The device waits for activation and receives connect info with deployment id and contract digest.

given: a device activation is in progress
when: the device waits for activation to complete
then: the device receives connect info with deployment id
the device receives connect info with contract digest

cases.title: Activated device connects and authenticates
cases.description: The activated device connects through the public device client surface and Auth.Sessions.Me reports participant kind device with expected identity.

given: a device has been activated
when: the device connects through the public device client surface
then: Auth.Sessions.Me reports participant kind device
Auth.Sessions.Me reports the expected identity

cases.title: Activated device appears in admin authority list
cases.description: The admin lists device user authorities and the activated device entry is present.

given: a device has been activated
when: the admin lists device user authorities
then: the list includes the activated device entry

cases.title: SQL outbox commits event through service-owned database transaction and delivers to NATS
cases.description: A service handler uses createSqlOutbox in an RPC handler, enqueues an event inside the transaction, and the event is published to NATS after the SQL transaction commits. The event is observable through a live event capture.

given: a service is connected with real SQLite and SQL outbox configured
the service RPC handler uses outbox.transaction to enqueue an event
when: the client calls Documents.Process
then: the RPC response includes the expected documentId
the event is captured by a live event subscriber

cases.title: SQL outbox suppresses events when the service transaction rolls back
cases.description: A service handler starts an outbox transaction, enqueues an event, then throws. The rollback ensures no event is published to NATS.

given: a service is connected with real SQLite and SQL outbox configured
the service RPC handler throws inside outbox.transaction
when: the client calls Documents.ProcessWithRollback
then: no event is captured by a live event subscriber

cases.title: SQL outbox publishes multiple events from a single transaction commit
cases.description: A service handler enqueues two different events in the same outbox transaction. Both events are published to NATS after the SQL transaction commits.

given: a service is connected with real SQLite and SQL outbox configured
the service RPC handler enqueues two events in one outbox.transaction
when: the client calls Documents.ProcessMultiEvent
then: both Document.Processed and Document.Audited events are captured
each event has the expected documentId payload

cases.title: Event listener derives a new event through SQL outbox
cases.description: A service registers an ephemeral event listener that uses outbox.transaction to enqueue a derived audit event. Both the original and derived events are published to NATS.

given: a service with an ephemeral event listener that derives an audited event through SQL outbox
when: the client calls Documents.Process
then: the Document.Processed event is captured
the Document.Audited event derived by the listener is captured

cases.title: Outbox SQL row is marked dispatched after successful commit
cases.description: After a successful outbox transaction commit, the outbox SQL row is marked with state 'dispatched' and kind 'event.publish'.

given: a service with SQL outbox configured and real SQLite
when: the client calls Documents.Process
then: the SQL outbox row has state 'dispatched'
the SQL outbox row has kind 'event.publish'
